steps.title: Understanding what the x-intercept represents
steps.content: An x-intercept is where the graph crosses the x-axis, which occurs when $y = 0$. In this problem, $y$ is the diver's height above the water in meters. When $y = 0$, the height is zero—the diver is at the water's surface. Therefore, the x-intercept represents the moment the diver hits the water.

steps.title: Reading the x-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The parabola crosses the x-axis at $x = 1.6$ seconds. This is the x-intercept, representing the time when the diver hits the water. That matches choice B — B is correct.
